name: Via Verde Llobregat - Berga
region_intro: Mountain biking around Berga offers diverse landscapes, from the pre-Pyrenean foothills to higher alpine terrain. The region features extensive trail networks utilizing old work routes, former mining tracks, and traditional mountain paths. Riders can experience varied terrain, including technical, rocky south faces and more humid, lush north faces. The altitude ranges significantly, providing a wide array of options for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails in Berga The most popular mountain bike trail is Via Verde Llobregat - Berga, an easy 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This route follows historical railway tracks, offering a gentle gradient. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sant Quintí de Montclar MTB Route, a moderate 16.8 miles (27.1 km) path. This trail provides diverse terrain and scenic views of the surrounding landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Obiols Trail, a 12.0 miles (19.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Berga is defined by the Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park, the iconic Pedraforca Massif, and the scenic La Baells Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging alpine enduro trails. The routes in Berga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Berga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Berga?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails around Berga, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and challenging routes, with 10 easy, 45 moderate, and 28 difficult trails listed on komoot.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Berga?
A: Berga offers incredibly diverse terrain, ranging from the pre-Pyrenean foothills to higher alpine landscapes. You can expect technical, rocky south faces, more humid and lush north faces, and trails that utilize old work routes, former mining tracks, and traditional mountain paths. The altitude varies significantly, from 500 meters to peaks of 2500 meters.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Berga?
A: Yes, Berga has several options suitable for beginners and families. For a gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958725618/via-verde-llobregat-berga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde Llobregat - Berga&lt;/a&gt;, which follows historical railway tracks with a gentle gradient. This easy 6.7-mile (10.8 km) trail is perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes in the Berga region?
A: Many routes in Berga offer stunning scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961714612/sant-quinti-de-montclar-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Quintí de Montclar MTB Route&lt;/a&gt; provides diverse terrain and scenic views. For panoramic vistas, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, known as the 'balcony of Catalonia', or loops around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4150630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Negra&lt;/a&gt; with views of the La Baells Reservoir.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Berga?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; offering breathtaking views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3786081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Peguera&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a glimpse into the region's mining history. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3420612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrat or the Figuerassa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1995635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi de les tres branques&lt;/a&gt; natural monument.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Berga?
A: Absolutely. Berga is known for its challenging alpine enduro trails. For experienced riders seeking significant elevation gain and technical sections, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24565187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Vicenç d'Obiols Hermitage – Llobregat Greenway Bridge loop from Berga&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 34.3-mile (55.2 km) trail, offer a demanding experience. The area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/840166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rasos de Peguera&lt;/a&gt; also presents a spectacular and challenging climb.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Berga?
A: Berga offers year-round biking opportunities due to its significant altitude range. However, for the best experience, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and stable weather. Higher elevations may experience snow in winter, while summer can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons ideal for rides.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Berga?
A: The mountain biking trails in Berga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the pre-Pyrenean and alpine landscapes, and the extensive network of trails that cater to all skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Berga?
A: Yes, there are many circular routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31446426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Negra – View of La Baells Reservoir loop from Berga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 16.1-mile (25.9 km) option. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961549504/obiols-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obiols Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a moderate circular route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural parks or protected areas can I explore by mountain bike near Berga?
A: A major highlight for mountain bikers is the expansive Cadí-Moixeró Natural Park. This park, covering over 40,000 hectares, offers over 400 km of marked trails and encompasses the impressive Cadí and Moixeró mountain ranges, including the emblematic Pedraforca Massif. It's a biodiversity hotspot with stunning alpine scenery.

Q: Is it possible to find bike repair services in Berga?
A: While specific bike repair shops aren't listed in the provided data, as a popular mountain biking destination, Berga likely has local services or sports shops that can assist with basic repairs and gear. It's always a good idea to carry a basic repair kit for minor issues on the trail.

Q: How accessible is Berga for mountain biking, especially regarding public transport or parking?
A: Berga is quite accessible, being just over an hour from Barcelona airport, making it an easily reachable destination. While specific parking details for trailheads are not provided, the region's popularity for outdoor activities suggests that parking facilities are generally available near popular starting points. For public transport, local bus services connect Berga to nearby towns and cities.
tips: Near the Peirota tunnel is the Peirotes or Vilarrassa bridge, made up of a structure over 50 meters long and approximately 3.75 meters wide.

Dating back to 1286, this bridge helped improve communications between Berga and the monastery of Sant Pere de la Portella. At first the bridge was made of wood, but a short time later it was rebuilt in stone and in the Gothic style. The central ogive as seen now was added in the 15th century.

This bridge is a magnificent example of medieval "cornered" or "donkey's back" bridges, with its central part raised, thus allowing a wider central arch that allows greater water flow. Three smaller ones are added to the sides, two to the right and one to the left, semicircular, somewhat irregular and lowered.

The bridge has undergone repairs and reconstructions over time due to floods, and in the 17th and 18th centuries the road was paved with boulders and a stone railing was added.

There used to be an old train bridge here, but it's gone. Now there is a small simple pedestrian bridge.
The trail after this one is a mix of trails that could be difficult on a bike, but there are some beautiful sections of the river.

waypoints.name: Old Pedestrian Bridge on the Llobregat Greenway near Berga
